.ddx/attachments/ddx-c2a66f45-wt-6bee7238/events.jsonl:1:{"actor":"erik","body":"{\"rationale\":\"Strong bead overall: title is imperative and names subsystem+change; description carries explicit PROBLEM / ROOT CAUSE with file:line refs / PROPOSED FIX / OUT-OF-SCOPE; AC is numbered (14 items), names Test* functions (AC7, AC8, AC12), and closes with the required `go test ./cmd/...` gate (AC13) and `lefthook run pre-commit` gate (AC14); labels carry phase:2, area:*, kind:feature, and spec:FEAT-011 cross-ref; parent and deps are explicit; no /tmp or chat references; standalone. Three concerns prevent an 8: (1) AC9 uses 'or' ('via ddx init/update behavior or an explicit repo-local sync command documented in the change'), giving the agent two code paths with no tie-breaker — it may pick the wrong one or produce a half-implementation; (2) AC10 references `make skill-schema` which may not exist in cli/Makefile — if absent the AC silently fails rather than guiding the agent to create or substitute it; (3) AC11 invokes `ddx skills check` with five positional paths — this multi-path signature should be verified against the current CLI surface before dispatch, as an unrecognised flag or arity mismatch will block the gate without a clear fix path.\",\"score\":7,\"suggested_fixes\":[\"AC9: collapse the 'or' to a single deterministic sequence, e.g. 'run make copy-skills to sync cli/internal/skills/ddx, then run ddx update to refresh .agents/skills/ddx, .claude/skills/ddx, and .ddx/skills/ddx; document both steps in the PR description.'\",\"AC10: before dispatch, confirm that `make skill-schema` exists in cli/Makefile; if absent, inline the equivalent invocation (e.g. `cd cli \\u0026\\u0026 go run ./cmd/ddx skill schema skills/ddx/SKILL.md`) so the AC is self-contained and cannot silently pass.\",\"AC11: verify that `ddx skills check` accepts multiple positional path arguments in the current CLI; if not, document the correct per-path invocation or replace with a loop so the AC is runnable verbatim.\"],\"waivers_applied\":[]}","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","kind":"bead-quality.lint","source":"ddx agent work","summary":"score=7"}

cli/internal/agent/testdata/reviewer/reviewer-stream-oversized-body.jsonl:1:{"id":"as-bc4b0e90","timestamp":"2026-04-15T01:50:16.151938804Z","harness":"claude","surface":"claude","canonical_target":"claude-sonnet-4-6","model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","prompt_len":13868,"prompt":"\u003cexecute-bead\u003e\n  \u003cbead id=\"axon-dccbdf73\"\u003e\n    \u003ctitle\u003eauth-jwt-pipeline: sign + verify with revocation cache + request extension\u003c/title\u003e\n    \u003cdescription\u003e\nImplement the full JWT verification middleware in axon-server.\n\n## What you're building\n\nAn axum middleware layer that runs on every data-plane request. It validates the JWT, installs a (user_id, tenant_id, grants) tuple into the request extension, and returns a typed AuthError on any failure. Downstream handlers read the extension and trust it completely.\n\n## Scope — exactly what to add\n\n### New file: `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`\n\nA module exposing two things:\n\n1. A `JwtIssuer` helper that signs a `JwtClaims` into a compact JWT string. HS256 is the only algorithm required for this bead. The signing key is a `Vec\u003cu8\u003e` passed in at construction. Provide `issue(\u0026self, claims: \u0026JwtClaims) -\u003e Result\u003cString, AuthError\u003e` and a `verify(\u0026self, token: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003cJwtClaims, AuthError\u003e` for round-tripping in tests.\n\n2. An axum middleware `async fn jwt_verify_layer` that runs the 8-step verification order below. On success, installs a `ResolvedIdentity { user_id, tenant_id, grants }` into the request extension. On failure, returns a response built via `AuthError::into_response()` so the mapping to HTTP status + error.code is consistent.\n\nThe middleware also owns an `InMemoryRevocationCache` (a small `tokio::sync::RwLock\u003cHashSet\u003cUuid\u003e\u003e` is fine; nothing fancy) and falls back to SQL (via the storage adapter's new `is_jti_revoked` method) on cache miss.\n\n### Extend: `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs`\n\n- Add `ResolvedIdentity { pub user_id: UserId, pub tenant_id: TenantId, pub grants: Grants }`.\n- Add `impl AuthError` with `pub fn status_code(\u0026self) -\u003e u16` returning 401 or 403 per ADR-018 §4 failure table, and `pub fn error_code(\u0026self) -\u003e \u0026'static str` returning the stable code string. (An axum `IntoResponse` impl can live in axon-server.)\n- Add `impl Grants` with `pub fn find_database(\u0026self, name: \u0026str) -\u003e Option\u003c\u0026GrantedDatabase\u003e` and `impl GrantedDatabase` with `pub fn has_op(\u0026self, op: Op) -\u003e bool`.\n\n### Extend: `crates/axon-storage/src/adapter.rs` (trait)\n\nAdd one method to the `StorageAdapter` trait:\n\n```rust\nasync fn is_jti_revoked(\u0026self, jti: Uuid) -\u003e Result\u003cbool, AxonError\u003e;\n```\n\nImplement it in `crates/axon-storage/src/memory.rs` (store a `HashSet\u003cUuid\u003e`; default-empty), `crates/axon-storage/src/sqlite.rs`, and `crates/axon-storage/src/postgres.rs` (`SELECT 1 FROM credential_revocations WHERE jti = $1`). The A1 schema (`auth_schema.rs`) already has the `credential_revocations` table — just query it.\n\n### Wire into: `crates/axon-server/src/lib.rs`\n\nRe-export `auth_pipeline::{jwt_verify_layer, JwtIssuer, InMemoryRevocationCache}`. Don't wire it into the main router yet — that's D-phase work. For this bead, the middleware just needs to exist and be testable in isolation.\n\n## Verification order (normative — ADR-018 §4)\n\n1. Extract `Authorization: Bearer \u003cjwt\u003e` from the request. Missing header → `AuthError::Unauthenticated` (401). The `--no-auth` bypass is NOT part of this bead — that's E2.\n2. Parse and verify the JWT signature with HS256 against the issuer's key. Signature invalid → `CredentialInvalid` (401). Malformed structure → `CredentialMalformed` (401). `aud` as a JSON array → `CredentialMalformed` (this falls out of the A2 `parse_claims` wrapper — use it).\n3. Check `nbf` and `exp` against `SystemTime::now()` with a 30s skew budget. `exp` more than 30s in the past → `CredentialExpired` (401). `nbf` more than 30s in the future → `CredentialNotYetValid` (401).\n4. Check `claims.jti` against the revocation cache, then SQL on miss. Hit → `CredentialRevoked` (401). Populate the cache on SQL hit to speed up subsequent checks.\n5. Compare `claims.aud` to the `{tenant}` segment extracted from the URL path. Mismatch → `CredentialWrongTenant` (**403**, not 401 — see the table). The URL extraction path is: look at `request.uri().path()` and parse the leading `/tenants/{tenant}/databases/{database}/...` segments. For this bead, implement a small helper `fn extract_tenant_database(path: \u0026str) -\u003e Option\u003c(\u0026str, \u0026str)\u003e` that returns `None` for non-data-plane paths so they skip the middleware.\n6. Look up the `sub` UUID in the `users` table via a new `StorageAdapter::get_user(UserId) -\u003e Result\u003cOption\u003cUser\u003e, AxonError\u003e` method. User not found or `user.suspended_at_ms.is_some()` → `UserSuspended` (401).\n7. Check tenant membership: a new `StorageAdapter::get_tenant_member(TenantId, UserId) -\u003e Result\u003cOption\u003cTenantMember\u003e, AxonError\u003e`. Missing → `NotATenantMember` (403).\n8. Walk `claims.grants.databases[]` looking for an entry matching the URL `{database}`. None → `DatabaseNotGranted` (403). Found → compute the required op from the HTTP method (GET = Read, POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE = Write) and intersect with the grant's ops. Empty intersection → `OpNotGranted` (403). Admin-requiring routes are out of scope for this bead — the middleware only handles Read/Write classification.\n9. Build `ResolvedIdentity` and install it via `request.extensions_mut().insert(resolved)`.\n\n## Error mapping — must exactly match ADR-018 §4\n\nThe mapping table is copied here verbatim. Do not invent variants; the 14 AuthError variants already exist in axon-core.\n\n| Failure | Status | `error.code` |\n|---|---|---|\n| No Authorization header | 401 | `unauthenticated` |\n| Header not `Bearer \u003ctoken\u003e` or base64 broken | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| JWT structurally invalid / missing claims | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| `aud` as JSON array | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| Signature invalid | 401 | `credential_invalid` |\n| `exp` in past (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_expired` |\n| `nbf` in future (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_not_yet_valid` |\n| `jti` revoked | 401 | `credential_revoked` |\n| `iss` unknown | 401 | `credential_foreign_issuer` — for this bead, reject if `claims.iss != issuer.issuer_id` |\n| `aud` ≠ URL tenant | **403** | `credential_wrong_tenant` |\n| `sub` suspended/deleted | 401 | `user_suspended` |\n| `sub` not a tenant member | **403** | `not_a_tenant_member` |\n| URL database not in grants | **403** | `database_not_granted` |\n| Op not in grant | **403** | `op_not_granted` |\n\nNote the 401 vs 403 split: credential-level problems are 401, scope-level problems are 403.\n\n## Testing — all must pass for this bead to be complete\n\n### Unit tests in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_test.rs`\n\nFor every row of the error mapping table, build the minimal defective request and assert the middleware produces exactly that `(status, error_code)` pair. Table-driven with `#[test]` or a single `#[test]` that iterates an array of cases.\n\nAdditionally:\n\n- `jwt_roundtrip_succeeds`: issue a valid JWT with `JwtIssuer::issue`, verify it with `JwtIssuer::verify`, assert claims match.\n- `valid_jwt_populates_request_extension`: run the full middleware on a valid JWT, assert the response is 200 and the handler sees `ResolvedIdentity` in extensions.\n- `revocation_takes_effect_within_one_second`: issue a valid JWT, verify it succeeds, insert the jti into the revocation cache, verify the next request returns 401 `credential_revoked`. Do NOT use sleep — invalidate the cache directly.\n- `clock_skew_30s_accepted_31s_rejected`: construct a JWT with `exp` at `now - 29s` (should succeed) and one with `exp` at `now - 31s` (should fail with `credential_expired`). Do the same for `nbf`.\n- `ops_matrix`: for each HTTP method in {GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE} and each op set in the grant, assert the expected 200/403 outcome.\n\n### Integration test in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_integration_test.rs`\n\nBuild a minimal axum router with one dummy route `GET /tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping`. Install the middleware. Hit it with:\n- No auth header → 401 `unauthenticated`\n- Valid JWT with `grants: [{name: \"orders\", ops: [read]}]` → 200\n- Valid JWT with the wrong tenant in aud → 403 `credential_wrong_tenant`\n- Valid JWT with grants for a different database → 403 `database_not_granted`\n\n### axon-sim workload `inv_018_grant_enforcement`\n\n**If this is too large for one pass, skip the axon-sim workload and note it in the commit message for a follow-up bead.** The core verification must ship; the simulation workload can follow. Do not block the whole bead on the workload.\n\n## Crate dependencies you will need\n\nAdd to `crates/axon-server/Cargo.toml` under `[dependencies]`:\n```toml\njsonwebtoken = \"9\"\n```\n\nEverything else (axum, tower, tokio, uuid, serde_json, http) is already in the workspace.\n\n## Context you must know about\n\n### A1 landed: schema\n- `crates/axon-storage/src/auth_schema.rs` has the SQL for `users`, `user_identities`, `tenant_users`, `credential_revocations`, etc.\n- `user_identities.external_id` (NOT `subject`) — already corrected.\n- Migration runs via `apply_auth_migrations_sqlite` / `_postgres`.\n\n### A2 landed: types\n- `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs` has all the types: `TenantId`, `UserId`, `User`, `UserIdentity`, `Tenant`, `TenantMember`, `TenantRole`, `Op`, `GrantedDatabase`, `Grants`, `JwtClaims`, `AuthError` (14 variants).\n- `parse_claims(\u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003cJwtClaims, AuthError\u003e` wraps serde_json and maps errors to `CredentialMalformed`. USE IT.\n- `AUTH_ERROR_VARIANT_COUNT == 14` is enforced via compile-time exhaustive match.\n- Delegation helper on `TenantRole`: `admin.can_delegate(op)` / etc.\n\n### Files you should NOT touch\n- Anything outside `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`, `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs` (additive), `crates/axon-storage/src/{adapter,memory,sqlite,postgres}.rs` (additive), and the two new test files.\n- The existing `crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs` — that's the Tailscale whois path. Leave it alone. Your pipeline is a new module.\n\n## Quality bar\n\nYou are the implementation step. A post-merge review with opus will verify the result against the AC. If your commit misses an AC item, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher power class. **Don't commit partial work hoping the review slides.** If you cannot complete the bead in this pass, write a `no_changes_rationale.txt` explaining exactly what's done and what's blocking, and do NOT commit — the bead will be re-queued.\n\nSpecifically: if you cannot make `cargo test -p axon-server` and `cargo clippy -p axon-server -- -D warnings` green, do not commit. SQLite connection.\\n15\\t///\\n16\\t/// This is idempotent — running it multiple times on the same database\\n17\\t/// produces the same result without errors or duplicate rows.\\n18\\tpub fn apply_auth_migrations_sqlite(conn: \u0026rusqlite::Connection) -\u003e Result\u003c(), String\u003e {\\n19\\t    // Create tenants table.\\n20\\t    // UNIQUE(name) enforced by the UNIQUE constraint on the name column.\\n21\\t    conn.execute_batch(\\n22\\t        \\\"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tenants (\\n23\\t            id                 TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\n24\\t            name               TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,\\n25\\t            display_name       TEXT NOT NULL,\\n26\\t            created_at_ms      INTEGER NOT NULL,\\n27\\t            updated_at_ms      INTEGER NOT NULL\\n28\\t        )\\\",\\n29\\t    )\\n30\\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\\\"tenants table: {e}\\\"))?;\\n31\\t\\n32\\t    // Create users table.\\n33\\t    conn.execute_batch(\\n34\\t        \\\"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (\\n35\\t            id                 TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\n36\\t            display_name       TEXT NOT NULL,\\n37\\t            email              TEXT,\\n38\\t            created_at_ms      INTEGER NOT NULL,\\n39\\t            suspended_at_ms    INTEGER\\n40\\t        )\\\",\\n41\\t    )\\n42\\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\\\"users table: {e}\\\"))?;\\n43\\t\\n44\\t    // Create user_identities table.\\n45\\t    // PRIMARY KEY (provider, external_id) enforces UNIQUE(provider, external_id).\\n46\\t    conn.execute_batch(\\n47\\t        \\\"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_identities (\\n48\\t            provider       TEXT NOT NULL,\\n49\\t            external_id    TEXT NOT NULL,\\n50\\t            user_id        TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),\\n51\\t            created_at_ms  INTEGER NOT NULL,\\n52\\t            PRIMARY KEY (provider, external_id)\\n53\\t        )\\\",\\n54\\t    )\\n55\\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\\\"user_identities table: {e}\\\"))?;\\n56\\t\\n57\\t    // Create tenant_users table.\\n58\\t    // PRIMARY KEY (tenant_id, user_id) is the composite PK required by ADR-018.\\n59\\t    conn.execute_batch(\\n60\\t        \\\"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tenant_users (\\n61\\t            tenant_id    TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(id),\\n62\\t            user_id      TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),\\n63\\t            role         TEXT NOT NULL,\\n64\\t            added_at_ms  INTEGER NOT NULL,\\n65\\t            PRIMARY KEY (tenant_id, user_id)\\n66\\t        )\\\",\\n67\\t    )\\n68\\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\\\"tenant_users table: {e}\\\"))?;\\n69\\t\\n70\\t    // Create tenant_databases table.\\n71\\t    conn.execute_batch(\\n72\\t        \\\"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tenant_databases (\\n73\\t            tenant_id      TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(id),\\n74\\t            database_name  TEXT NOT NULL,\\n75\\t            created_at_ms  INTEGER NOT NULL,\\n76\\t            PRIMARY KEY Axon server — HTTP gateway, gRPC service, MCP stdio, and authentication.\\n2\\t//!\\n3\\t//! # Authentication\\n4\\t//!\\n5\\t//! Identity is resolved once per request by [`auth::AuthContext`] and\\n6\\t//! injected as a typed extension.  Three modes are supported:\\n7\\t//!\\n8\\t//! | Mode | Flag | Actor | Role |\\n9\\t//! |------|------|-------|------|\\n10\\t//! | `NoAuth` | `--no-auth` | `\\\"anonymous\\\"` | Admin |\\n11\\t//! | `Tailscale` | *(default)* | node name | from ACL tags |\\n12\\t//! | `Guest` | `--guest-role \u003crole\u003e` | `\\\"guest\\\"` | configured role |\\n13\\t//!\\n14\\t//! In `Tailscale` mode the server contacts the local Tailscale daemon over its\\n15\\t//! Unix socket (`/run/tailscale/tailscaled.sock` by default) and calls\\n16\\t//! `/localapi/v0/whois?addr=\u003cpeer\u003e` to resolve the connecting node's identity.\\n17\\t//! Resolved identities are cached by peer IP for the duration of\\n18\\t//! `--auth-cache-ttl-secs` (default 60 s).\\n19\\t//!\\n20\\t//! ACL tag → role mapping:\\n21\\t//! - `tag:axon-admin` → [`Role::Admin`]\\n22\\t//! - `tag:axon-write` / `tag:axon-agent` → [`Role::Write`]\\n23\\t//! - `tag:axon-read` → [`Role::Read`]\\n24\\t//! - no matching tag → `--tailscale-default-role` (default `read`)\\n25\\t//!\\n26\\t//! Connections that are not on the tailnet are rejected with HTTP 401 / gRPC\\n27\\t//! `UNAUTHENTICATED`.  If the Tailscale daemon is unreachable the server\\n28\\t//! returns HTTP 503 / gRPC `UNAVAILABLE`.\\n29\\t//!\\n30\\t//! # Request flow\\n31\\t//!\\n32\\t//! ```text\\n33\\t//! TCP accept\\n34\\t//!   └─ authenticate_http_request  (gateway.rs middleware)\\n35\\t//!        ├─ AuthContext::resolve_peer  →  Identity\\n36\\t//!        └─ insert Identity into request extensions\\n37\\t//!             └─ route handler\\n38\\t//!                  ├─ extract Extension\u003cIdentity\u003e\\n39\\t//!                  ├─ identity.require_read() / require_write() / require_admin()\\n40\\t//!                  └─ handler logic\\n41\\t//! ```\\n42\\t//!\\n43\\t//! gRPC follows the same pattern via [`service::AxonServiceImpl::authorize`].\\n44\\tpub mod Role-based and attribute-based access control for Axon (FEAT-012).\\n1\\t//!\\n2\\t//! # Layers\\n3\\t//!\\n4\\t//! **Layer 1 — RBAC** ([`Role`], [`CallerIdentity`], [`Operation`])\\n5\\t//!\\n6\\t//! Four built-in roles (`Admin \u003e Write \u003e Read \u003e None`) control access to\\n7\\t//! Axon operations.  Roles are derived from the identity provider (Tailscale\\n8\\t//! ACL tags, OIDC claims, etc.) by the server layer and passed into handlers\\n9\\t//! as a [`CallerIdentity`].  Handlers call [`CallerIdentity::check`] to\\n10\\t//! enforce the minimum required role.\\n11\\t//!\\n12\\t//! **Layer 2 — Field-level masking** ([`MaskPolicy`])\\n13\\t//!\\n14\\t//! A `MaskPolicy` hides individual entity fields from callers whose role\\n15\\t//! falls below a configured minimum.  Applied by [`CallerIdentity::apply_masks`]\\n16\\t//! before returning entity data to the caller.  Admins always see all fields.\\n17\\t//!\\n18\\t//! **Layer 3 — Collection write control** ([`WritePolicy`])\\n19\\t//!\\n20\\t//! A `WritePolicy` sets a per-collection minimum write role and marks fields\\n21\\t//! as immutable after creation.  Checked by the handler before any mutation.\\n22\\t//!\\n23\\t//! **Layer 4 — Database-scoped grants** ([`DatabaseGrant`], [`GrantRegistry`])\\n24\\t//!\\n25\\t//! Grants scope a role to a specific database (or `\\\"*\\\"` for all databases).\\n26\\t//! Without a matching grant a caller has `Role::None` on that database.\\n27\\t//! Admins with a global grant bypass per-database checks.  The grant registry\\n28\\t//! is stored in `__axon_policies__` and enforced at the routing layer.\\n29\\t\\n30\\tuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\\n31\\t\\n32\\tuse crate::error::AxonError;\\n33\\t\\n34\\t/// Built-in roles for Axon RBAC (FEAT-012).\\n35\\t///\\n36\\t/// Roles are ordered by privilege: `Admin \u003e Write \u003e Read \u003e None`.\\n37\\t/// When a user has multiple roles (e.g., from multiple ACL tags),\\n38\\t/// the highest-privilege role wins.\\n39\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]\\n40\\tpub enum Role {\\n41\\t    /// No access. Explicitly denied.\\n42\\t    None = 0,\\n43\\t    /// Read entities, query, traverse, browse audit log.\\n44\\t    Read = 1,\\n45\\t    /// Create, update, delete entities and links in any collection.\\n46\\t    Write = 2,\\n47\\t    /// All operations including drop and schema changes.\\n48\\t    Admin = 3,\\n49\\t}\\n50\\t\\n51\\timpl Role {\\n52\\t    /// Parse a role from a Tailscale ACL tag or string name.\\n53\\t    ///\\n54\\t    /// Accepted inputs (both full `tag:` prefix and bare name):\\n55\\t    ///\\n56\\t    /// | Input | Role |\\n57\\t    /// |-------|------|\\n58\\t    /// | `\\\"tag:axon-admin\\\"` / `\\\"tag:admin\\\"` / `\\\"admin\\\"` | `Admin` |\\n59\\t    /// | `\\\"tag:axon-write\\\"` / `\\\"tag:axon-agent\\\"` / `\\\"tag:write\\\"` / `\\\"write\\\"` | `Write` |\\n60\\t    /// | `\\\"tag:axon-read\\\"` / `\\\"tag:read\\\"` / `\\\"read\\\"` | `Read` |\\n61\\t    /// | anything else | `None` |\\n62\\t    ///\\n63\\t    /// `tag:axon-agent` is an alias for `write` — the conventional tag for\\n64\\t    /// automated agent workloads that need read/write but not admin access.\\n65\\t    pub fn from_tag(tag: \u0026str) -\u003e Self {\\n66\\t        match tag {\\n67\\t            \\\"tag:axon-admin\\\" | \\\"tag:admin\\\" | \\\"admin\\\" =\u003e Role::Admin,\\n68\\t            \\\"tag:axon-write\\\" | \\\"tag:axon-agent\\\" | \\\"tag:write\\\" | \\\"write\\\" =\u003e Role::Write,\\n69\\t            \\\"tag:axon-read\\\" | \\\"tag:read\\\" | \\\"read\\\" =\u003e Role::Read,\\n70\\t            _ =\u003e Role::None,\\n71\\t        }\\n72\\t    }\\n73\\t\\n74\\t    /// Determine the highest-privilege role from a set of tags.\\n75\\t    pub fn from_tags(tags: \u0026[String]) -\u003e Self {\\n76\\t        tags.iter()\\n77\\t            .map(|t| Role::from_tag(t))\\n78\\t            .max()\\n79\\t            .unwrap_or(Role::None)\\n80\\t    }\\n81\\t}\\n82\\t\\n83\\timpl std::fmt::Display for Role {\\n84\\t    fn fmt(\u0026self, f: \u0026mut std::fmt::Formatter\u003c'_\u003e) -\u003e std::fmt::Result {\\n85\\t        match self {\\n86\\t            Role::None =\u003e write!(f, \\\"none\\\"),\\n87\\t            Role::Read =\u003e write!(f, \\\"read\\\"),\\n88\\t            Role::Write =\u003e write!(f, \\\"write\\\"),\\n89\\t            Role::Admin =\u003e write!(f, \\\"admin\\\"),\\n90\\t        }\\n91\\t    }\\n92\\t}\\n93\\t\\n94\\t/// The kind of operation being attempted.\\n95\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\\n96\\tpub enum Operation {\\n97\\t    /// Read an entity, query, traverse, audit.\\n98\\t    Read,\\n99\\t    /// Create, update, delete entities/links.\\n100\\t    Write,\\n101\\t    /// Drop collections, modify schemas, manage namespaces.\\n102\\t    Admin,\\n103\\t}\\n104\\t\\n105\\timpl Operation {\\n106\\t    /// The minimum role required for this operation.\\n107\\t    pub fn required_role(self) -\u003e Role {\\n108\\t        match self {\\n109\\t            Operation::Read =\u003e Role::Read,\\n110\\t            Operation::Write =\u003e Role::Write,\\n111\\t            Operation::Admin =\u003e Role::Admin,\\n112\\t        }\\n113\\t    }\\n114\\t}\\n115\\t\\n116\\t/// A simple field-equality filter used as an agent scope constraint\\n117\\t/// (FEAT-022 / ADR-016).\\n118\\t///\\n119\\t/// When attached to a [`CallerIdentity`], the agent guardrails layer rejects\\n120\\t/// any mutation whose target entity data does not satisfy\\n121\\t/// `data[field] == value`. V1 supports only field-equality predicates;\\n122\\t/// compound and range filters are deferred.\\n123\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\\n124\\tpub struct EntityFilter {\\n125\\t    /// The top-level data field to compare.\\n126\\t    pub field: String,\\n127\\t    /// The required value for that field.\\n128\\t    pub value: serde_json::Value,\\n129\\t}\\n130\\t\\n131\\timpl EntityFilter {\\n132\\t    /// Convenience constructor.\\n133\\t    pub fn new(field: impl Into\u003cString\u003e, value: serde_json::Value) -\u003e Self {\\n134\\t        Self {\\n135\\t            field: field.into(),\\n136\\t            value,\\n137\\t        }\\n138\\t    }\\n139\\t\\n140\\t    /// Returns `true` when the JSON object `data` satisfies this filter.\\n141\\t    ///\\n142\\t    /// Returns `false` when `data` is not a JSON object, when the field is\\n143\\t    /// absent, or when the value does not equal [`Self::value`].\\n144\\t    pub fn matches(\u0026self, data: \u0026serde_json::Value) -\u003e bool {\\n145\\t        data.get(\u0026self.field).is_some_and(|v| v == \u0026self.value)\\n146\\t    }\\n147\\t}\\n148\\t\\n149\\t/// Identity context for a request.\\n150\\t///\\n151\\t/// Populated by the auth middleware from the identity provider.\\n152\\t/// In `--no-auth` mode, all requests get `CallerIdentity::anonymous()`.\\n153\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\\n154\\tpub struct CallerIdentity {\\n155\\t    /// Actor name for audit log entries.\\n156\\t    pub actor: String,\\n157\\t    /// The caller's effective role (highest privilege from all their tags).\\n158\\t    pub role: Role,\\n159\\t    /// Optional scope constraint enforced by the agent guardrails layer\\n160\\t    /// (FEAT-022, ADR-016). When `Some`, this caller may only mutate entities\\n161\\t    /// whose data matches the filter. `None` means no scope restriction.\\n162\\t    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \\\"Option::is_none\\\")]\\n163\\t    pub entity_filter: Option\u003cEntityFilter\u003e,\\n164\\t}\\n165\\t\\n166\\timpl CallerIdentity {\\n167\\t    /// Create an anonymous identity with admin privileges (`--no-auth` mode).\\n168\\t    pub fn anonymous() -\u003e Self {\\n169\\t        Self {\\n170\\t            actor: \\\"anonymous\\\".into(),\\n171\\t            role: Role::Admin,\\n172\\t            entity_filter: None,\\n173\\t        }\\n174\\t    }\\n175\\t\\n176\\t    /// Create an identity from a name and role.\\n177\\t    pub fn new(actor: impl Into\u003cString\u003e, role: Role) -\u003e Self {\\n178\\t        Self {\\n179\\t            actor: actor.into(),\\n180\\t            role,\\n181\\t            entity_filter: None,\\n182\\t        }\\n183\\t    }\\n184\\t\\n185\\t    /// Attach an entity scope filter (builder style, FEAT-022).\\n186\\t    pub fn with_entity_filter(mut self, filter: EntityFilter) -\u003e Self {\\n187\\t        self.entity_filter = Some(filter);\\n188\\t        self\\n189\\t    }\\n190\\t\\n191\\t    /// Check whether this caller is authorized for the given operation.\\n192\\t    ///\\n193\\t    /// Returns `Ok(())` if authorized, or `Err(AxonError::InvalidOperation)`\\n194\\t    /// with a descriptive message if not.\\n195\\t    pub fn check(\u0026self, op: Operation) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n196\\t        let required = op.required_role();\\n197\\t        if self.role \u003e= required {\\n198\\t            Ok(())\\n199\\t        } else {\\n200\\t            Err(AxonError::InvalidOperation(format!(\\n201\\t                \\\"permission denied: role '{}' cannot perform {:?} operations (requires '{}')\\\",\\n202\\t                self.role, op, required\\n203\\t            )))\\n204\\t        }\\n205\\t    }\\n206\\t\\n207\\t    /// Apply field-level masking to entity data based on mask policies.\\n208\\t    ///\\n209\\t    /// Admin users see all fields. For other roles, fields listed in a\\n210\\t    /// mask policy that requires a higher role than the caller's are\\n211\\t    /// removed from the data.\\n212\\t    pub fn apply_masks(\u0026self, data: \u0026mut serde_json::Value, policies: \u0026[MaskPolicy]) {\\n213\\t        if self.role \u003e= Role::Admin {\\n214\\t            return; // Admin sees all.\\n215\\t        }\\n216\\t        if let Some(obj) = data.as_object_mut() {\\n217\\t            for policy in policies {\\n218\\t                if self.role \u003c policy.min_role {\\n219\\t                    obj.remove(\u0026policy.field);\\n220\\t                }\\n221\\t            }\\n222\\t        }\\n223\\t    }\\n224\\t}\\n225\\t\\n226\\t/// A field-level mask policy (US-046, FEAT-012).\\n227\\t///\\n228\\t/// Specifies that a field should be hidden from callers whose role is\\n229\\t/// below `min_role`. Admin always sees all fields.\\n230\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\\n231\\tpub struct MaskPolicy {\\n232\\t    /// The field name to mask.\\n233\\t    pub field: String,\\n234\\t    /// Minimum role required to see this field.\\n235\\t    pub min_role: Role,\\n236\\t}\\n237\\t\\n238\\t// ── Attribute-based write control (US-047, FEAT-012) ────────────────────────\\n239\\t\\n240\\t/// Per-collection write policy for attribute-based access control (ABAC).\\n241\\t///\\n242\\t/// Stored in the `__axon_policies__` pseudo-collection. Defines:\\n243\\t/// - Minimum role required to write to this collection\\n244\\t/// - Fields that are immutable after creation\\n245\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\\n246\\tpub struct WritePolicy {\\n247\\t    /// Collection this policy applies to.\\n248\\t    pub collection: String,\\n249\\t    /// Minimum role required for write operations.\\n250\\t    pub min_write_role: Role,\\n251\\t    /// Fields that cannot be modified after the initial create.\\n252\\t    #[serde(default)]\\n253\\t    pub immutable_fields: Vec\u003cString\u003e,\\n254\\t}\\n255\\t\\n256\\timpl WritePolicy {\\n257\\t    /// Check if a caller has sufficient role to write to this collection.\\n258\\t    pub fn check_write(\u0026self, caller: \u0026CallerIdentity) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n259\\t        if caller.role \u003e= self.min_write_role {\\n260\\t            Ok(())\\n261\\t        } else {\\n262\\t            Err(AxonError::InvalidOperation(format!(\\n263\\t                \\\"write policy for '{}': role '{}' insufficient (requires '{}')\\\",\\n264\\t                self.collection, caller.role, self.min_write_role\\n265\\t            )))\\n266\\t        }\\n267\\t    }\\n268\\t\\n269\\t    /// Check if any immutable fields have been modified.\\n270\\t    ///\\n271\\t    /// Returns the list of field names that were illegally modified.\\n272\\t    pub fn check_immutable_fields(\\n273\\t        \u0026self,\\n274\\t        old_data: \u0026serde_json::Value,\\n275\\t        new_data: \u0026serde_json::Value,\\n276\\t    ) -\u003e Vec\u003cString\u003e {\\n277\\t        let mut violations = Vec::new();\\n278\\t        for field in \u0026self.immutable_fields {\\n279\\t            let old_val = old_data.get(field);\\n280\\t            let new_val = new_data.get(field);\\n281\\t            // If the field existed before and is now different or missing, it's a violation.\\n282\\t            if old_val.is_some() \u0026\u0026 old_val != new_val {\\n283\\t                violations.push(field.clone());\\n284\\t            }\\n285\\t        }\\n286\\t        violations\\n287\\t    }\\n288\\t}\\n289\\t\\n290\\t// ── Database-scoped access control (US-038, FEAT-014) ────────────────────────\\n291\\t\\n292\\t/// A grant that scopes a role to a specific database.\\n293\\t///\\n294\\t/// Without a grant, users cannot access a database (unless they have a\\n295\\t/// wildcard `*` grant or admin role).\\n296\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\\n297\\tpub struct DatabaseGrant {\\n298\\t    /// The actor (user/node) this grant applies to. `\\\"*\\\"` means all users.\\n299\\t    pub actor: String,\\n300\\t    /// The database name. `\\\"*\\\"` means all databases.\\n301\\t    pub database: String,\\n302\\t    /// The role granted for this database.\\n303\\t    pub role: Role,\\n304\\t}\\n305\\t\\n306\\t/// Registry of database-scoped grants.\\n307\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Serialize, Deserialize)]\\n308\\tpub struct GrantRegistry {\\n309\\t    grants: Vec\u003cDatabaseGrant\u003e,\\n310\\t}\\n311\\t\\n312\\timpl GrantRegistry {\\n313\\t    /// Create a new empty grant registry.\\n314\\t    pub fn new() -\u003e Self {\\n315\\t        Self::default()\\n316\\t    }\\n317\\t\\n318\\t    /// Add a grant.\\n319\\t    pub fn add(\u0026mut self, grant: DatabaseGrant) {\\n320\\t        self.grants.push(grant);\\n321\\t (`/docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-018-tenant-user-credential-model.md`)\\n\\nThis architectural decision document outlines a major multi-tenancy model for Axon:\\n\\n**Key Design Decisions:**\\n- **Tenant as isolation boundary**: A tenant is a customer account that may own multiple databases\\n- **Global user identities**: Users are stored globally and can be members of multiple tenants with different roles\\n- **M:N tenant-user relationship**: Many users per tenant, many tenants per user\\n- **JWT-based credentials**: Scoped, short-lived machine credentials with narrower grants than issuer role\\n- **Federated identity**: Support for Tailscale, OIDC, email/password via provider+external_id mapping\\n- **Path-based resource identity**: Canonical URLs for entities: `/database/{database}/entity/{entity_id}`\\n\\n**Core Entities:**\\n- `Tenant`: Account boundary (id, name, display_name, created_at_ms)\\n- `User`: Global identity (id, display_name, email, created_at_ms, suspended_at_ms)\\n- `UserIdentity`: Federated provider mapping (provider, external_id, user_id)\\n- `TenantMember`: M:N membership (tenant_id, user_id, role)\\n- `TenantRole`: {Admin, Write, Read} roles per tenant\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 2. **Auth Core Types** (`/crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs`)\\n\\n**Legacy RBAC Layer 1:**\\n- `Role`: {Admin, Write, Read, None} with privilege ordering\\n- `CallerIdentity`: Identity + role + optional entity filter\\n- `Operation`: {Read, Write, Admin} operations\\n- `MaskPolicy`: Field-level visibility based on role\\n- `WritePolicy`: Collection-level write control and immutable fields\\n- `DatabaseGrant` \u0026 `GrantRegistry`: Database-scoped role grants\\n\\n**ADR-018 Auth Types:**\\n- `TenantId`, `UserId`: Transparent newtype wrappers for IDs\\n- `Tenant`, `User`, `UserIdentity`, `TenantMember`: Main entities\\n- `TenantRole`: {Admin, Write, Read} with delegation helpers\\n- `Op`: {Read, Write, Admin} grantable operations\\n- `GrantedDatabase`: Named database with ops list\\n- `Grants`: Collection of granted databases (max 1024)\\n- `JwtClaims`: JWT payload with iss, sub, aud, jti, iat, nbf, exp, grants\\n- `AuthError`: 14 variants covering all JWT failure modes (per ADR-018 §4):\\n  - Unauthenticated, CredentialMalformed, CredentialInvalid, CredentialExpired\\n  - CredentialNotYetValid, CredentialRevoked, CredentialForeignIssuer, CredentialWrongTenant\\n  - UserSuspended, NotATenantMember, DatabaseNotGranted, OpNotGranted\\n  - GrantsExceedIssuerRole, GrantsMalformed\\n\\n**Validation:**\\n- `Grants::validate()` checks: ≤1024 databases, non-empty name/ops per database, unique ops\\n- `JwtClaims::validate()` ensures grants structure is valid\\n- Compile-time exhaustiveness check on AuthError variants (const fn match)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 3. **Auth Schema (SQL Migrations)** (`/crates/axon-storage/src/auth_schema.rs`)\\n\\n**6 Tables Created:**\\n1. **tenants**: id (TEXT PK), name (TEXT UNIQUE), display_name, created_at_ms, updated_at_ms\\n2. **users**: id (TEXT PK), display_name, email (NULLABLE), created_at_ms, suspended_at_ms (NULLABLE)\\n3. **user_identities**: (provider, external_id) PK, user_id FK, created_at_ms\\n4. **tenant_users**: (tenant_id, user_id) PK, role, added_at_ms\\n5. **tenant_databases**: (tenant_id, database_name) PK, created_at_ms\\n6. **credential_revocations**: jti (TEXT PK), revoked_at_ms, revoked_by (NULLABLE)\\n\\n**Implementation:**\\n- Both SQLite (via `rusqlite`) and PostgreSQL (via `tokio_postgres`) support\\n- Idempotent migrations (IF NOT EXISTS)\\n- Comprehensive round-trip tests for each table\\n- Constraint tests (unique tenant names, user identities, tenant_user composite PK)\\n- Tests explicitly verify `audit_retention_policies` is NOT created here (F1 responsibility)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 4. **Storage Adapter Trait** (`/crates/axon-storage/src/adapter.rs`)\\n\\n**Core Entity Operations:**\\n- `get`, `put`, `delete`, `count`, `range_scan`\\n- `compare_and_swap` (OCC versioning)\\n- `create_if_absent` (recovery/rollback)\\n\\n**Transaction Support:**\\n- `begin_tx`, `commit_tx`, `abort_tx` (default no-op)\\n- `append_audit_entry` (co-located audit writes)\\n\\n**Catalog Management (FEAT-014):**\\n- `create_database`, `list_databases`, `drop_database`\\n- `create_namespace`, `list_namespaces`, `drop_namespace`\\n- `list_namespace_collections`, `register_collection`, `unregister_collection`, `list_collections`\\n\\n**Schema Persistence:**\\n- `put_schema`, `get_schema`, `get_schema_version`, `list_schema_versions`, `delete_schema`\\n\\n**Collection Views:**\\n- `put_collection_view`, `get_collection_view`, `delete_collection_view`\\n\\n**Secondary Indexes (FEAT-013):**\\n- `update_indexes`, `remove_index_entries`\\n- `index_lookup`, `index_range`, `index_unique_conflict`\\n- `drop_indexes`\\n\\n**Compound Indexes (US-033):**\\n- `update_compound_indexes`, `remove_compound_index_entries`\\n- `compound_index_lookup`, `compound_index_prefix`\\n\\n**Link Management (ADR-010):**\\n- `put_link`, `delete_link`, `get_link`\\n- `list_outbound_links`, `list_inbound_links`\\n- `purge_links_for_collections`\\n\\n**Numeric Collection IDs (ADR-010):**\\n- `collection_numeric_id`, `collection_by_numeric_id`\\n\\n**Helper Types:**\\n- `IndexValue`: {String, Integer, Float(OrderedFloat), Boolean}\\n- `OrderedFloat`: Total-ordering f64 wrapper for BTreeMap keys\\n- `CompoundKey`: Vec of IndexValue with lexicographic Ord\\n- Functions: `extract_index_value`, `extract_compound_key`, `resolve_field_path`\\n\\n**Blanket Impl:** `Box\u003cdyn StorageAdapter\u003e` forwards all methods for vtable dispatch\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 5. **In-Memory Storage Adapter** (`/crates/axon-storage/src/memory.rs`)\\n\\n**State:**\\n- `data`: HashMap\u003cCatalogKey, HashMap\u003cEntityId, Entity\u003e\u003e — actual data\\n- `schema_versions`: Full history per collection (BTreeMap\u003cversion, SchemaVersionEntry\u003e)\\n- `collection_views`: Latest view per collection\\n- `collections`: Set of registered collection catalog keys\\n- `databases`, `namespaces`: Catalog structure\\n- `tx_snapshot`: Full snapshot captured at begin_tx (for rollback)\\n- `indexes`: EAV index (collection, field, value) → set of entity IDs\\n- `compound_indexes`: (collection, index_position, compound_key) → set of entity IDs\\n- `numeric_ids`: NumericIdCache (bidirectional name-to-numeric-ID mapping)\\n- `links`: Dedicated link store (source_col, source_id, link_type, target_col, target_id) → Link\\n\\n**Key Features:**\\n- Full snapshot-based transactions (isolation via cloning on begin_tx)\\n- Namespace-aware catalog resolution (default.default for unqualified names)\\n- Index enforcement (unique constraint violations on update_indexes)\\n- Comprehensive test suite (2400+ lines): entity CRUD, transactions, schemas, indexes, compound indexes, numeric IDs, links, namespaces\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 6. **Server Library** (`/crates/axon-server/src/lib.rs`)\\n\\n**Public Exports:**\\n- `auth`: AuthContext, AuthMode, Identity, Role\\n- `mcp_stdio`: run_mcp_stdio\\n- `service`: AxonServiceImpl\\n\\n**Authentication Flow (documented):**\\n- Three modes: NoAuth (--no-auth), Tailscale (default), Guest (--guest-role)\\n- Tailscale: resolves node identity via local daemon's `/localapi/v0/whois`, maps ACL tags to roles\\n- 60s default cache TTL for resolved identities\\n- ACL tag → role mapping: tag:axon-admin → Admin, tag:axon-{write,agent} → Write, tag:axon-read → Read\\n- HTTP 401 / gRPC UNAUTHENTICATED on auth failure\\n- HTTP 503 / gRPC UNAVAILABLE if Tailscale daemon unreachable\\n\\n**Request Pipeline:**\\n1. TCP accept\\n2. authenticate_http_request middleware (gateway.rs)\\n3. AuthContext::resolve_peer → Identity\\n4. Extract Extension\u003cIdentity\u003e in handler\\n5. Call identity.require_{read,write,admin}()\\n6. Handler logic\\n\\n**Internal Modules:**\\n- `actor_scope`: Agent scope constraints\\n- `auth`: Authentication context and resolution\\n- `control_plane`, `control_plane_routes`: Management API\\n- `cors_config`: CORS setup\\n- `user_roles`: Role definitions\\n- `gateway`: HTTP middleware\\n- `idempotency`: Idempotent request handling\\n- `mcp_http`, `mcp_stdio`: MCP protocol support\\n- `rate_limit`: Rate limiting\\n- `schema_registry`: Schema management\\n- `serve`: Server startup\\n- `service`: gRPC service implementation\\n- `tenant_router`: Tenant-based routing\\n- `embedded_ui`: Bundled UI assets\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 7. **Cargo Manifests**\\n\\n**axon-server/Cargo.toml:**\\n- Depends on all core crates: axon-core, axon-api, axon-audit, axon-schema, axon-storage, axon-mcp, .ddx/attachments/ddx-5bac3c6b/events.jsonl:1:{"actor":"audit-reviewer","body":"The parallel-agent review-loop epic bead ddx-5bac3c6b asks for ddx agent doctor to probe provider backends. However, the user explicitly scoped doctor vs check differently in the agent-routing visibility epic:\n\n- doctor = \"is my config failed?\" (config validation — static)\n- check = \"what agents can I use right now?\" (runtime availability — dynamic)\n\nVisibility 5 ddx-c4e3d91b landed the new \"ddx agent check [--provider X]\" command that does exactly what 5bac3c6b requests: probe each provider in real time via the /v1/models endpoint, report per-provider liveness, exit non-zero if no provider is reachable. Commit: f0f237c.\n\nClosing 5bac3c6b as superseded. The functional requirement is met; the architectural placement is different from what 5bac3c6b proposed, but the user preference (doctor vs check separation) is the correct split.\n\nIf the bead-authoring team wants doctor to ALSO nag-hint \"also run ddx agent check\" when its harness-binary-only check passes, that is a trivial follow-up bead but not what 5bac3c6b originally demanded.","created_at":"2026-04-15T03:45:01.82537177Z","kind":"note","source":"open-bead audit 2026-04-15","summary":"SUPERSEDED by ddx-c4e3d91b (Visibility 5: ddx agent check). The runtime provider-liveness probe that this bead requests is the core purpose of the new \"ddx agent check\" command, which already landed. Doctor is deliberately scoped to config validation only."}

.ddx/attachments/ddx-ce7a796e/events.jsonl:3:{"actor":"ddx","body":"no_changes\nrationale: Bead ddx-ce7a796e (B22d-g) cannot be landed in isolation against base\n41d464b03c4e5d12b7a9244c3c59ee312e64e255. Same fundamental blocker as\nB22d-f (ddx-9f4b1a8c, no_changes 2026-04-27): lefthook `runtime-lint`\nhook (lefthook.yml) filters analyzer diagnostics to the set of staged\nfiles, and the in-scope test files contain pre-existing RunOptions /\nCompareOptions composite literals whose underlying APIs are explicitly\nnamed out-of-scope by this bead's parent decomposition (step h owns\nservice_run.go; runner.go and claude_stream.go aren't in this step's\nin-scope at all).\n\nWhat is done (verified locally, then reverted)\n- compare_adapter.go is straightforward to drain of RunOptions /\n  CompareOptions / QuorumOptions:\n    * RunFunc redeclared as `func(rcfg config.ResolvedConfig,\n      runtime AgentRunRuntime) (*Result, error)`.\n    * defaultResolvePromptForCompare(runtime AgentRunRuntime).\n    * runCompareArmWith(run RunFunc, rcfg, runtime CompareRuntime,\n      armIdx, harnessName, baseDir, prompt, worktreePath).\n    * RunCompareWith(run, rcfg, runtime CompareRuntime, resolvePrompt,\n      cleanupSandbox).\n    * RunQuorumWith(run, rcfg, runtime QuorumRuntime).\n    * RunBenchmarkWith(runCompare func(config.ResolvedConfig,\n      CompareRuntime) (*ComparisonRecord, error), suite).\n    * RunCompareViaService / RunCompareWithAgent / RunQuorumViaService /\n      RunQuorumWithAgent / RunBenchmarkViaService / RunBenchmarkWithAgent\n      rewritten to take (rcfg, runtime) instead of CompareOptions /\n      QuorumOptions and to delegate through the new RunFunc.\n    * RunCompareWithConfigViaService / RunQuorumWithConfigViaService\n      collapsed into a single forwarding hop (they no longer need to\n      assemble a CompareOptions/QuorumOptions intermediary).\n    * RunQuorumWithConfig retained, now calling RunQuorumWith with the\n      new shape.\n- models.go BenchmarkArmsToCompare changes return shape from\n  CompareOptions to CompareRuntime (and accepts an AgentRunRuntime\n  baseline rather than a RunOptions). compare_adapter.go's\n  RunBenchmarkWith uses that shape directly.\n- models_test.go updates the BenchmarkArmsToCompare call to pass\n  `AgentRunRuntime{Prompt: \"test\"}` and assert\n  CompareRuntime.{Harnesses, ArmModels, ArmLabels}. This is the only\n  RunOptions-related drift in models_test.go — the rest of that file\n  doesn't reference RunOptions.\n- After the in-package edits, `cd cli && go build ./...` passes and\n  `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/...` is clean once the test\n  files in the next bullet are also updated.\n- agent_test.go (out of scope) needs one closure-shape update at\n  line 584 because RunFunc's signature changed; with that one-line\n  diff applied locally, TestQuorumRunsAllHarnesses passes.\n\nWhat is blocking (lint policy vs. bead scope)\n\n1. claude_stream_test.go (in-scope) contains RunOptions composite\n   literals at lines 206, 212, 218, 224, 291, and 324. These are all\n   inside test cases that call helpers declared in claude_stream.go —\n   resolveClaudeProgressLogDir(opts RunOptions, cfg Config) at\n   claude_stream.go:284 and runClaudeStreamingFn(r, ctx, harness,\n   \"claude\", \"\", opts RunOptions, \"hi\", \"\", 10*time.Second) at\n   claude_stream.go:304/477/507. claude_stream.go is NOT in this\n   bead's in-scope; B22d's per-step decomposition reserves it for a\n   separate step. With claude_stream.go's RunOptions surface\n   unchanged, the test cannot construct anything other than\n   RunOptions{...} to drive resolveClaudeProgressLogDir, and the\n   runtime-lint hook flags every one of those literals when the\n   file is staged.\n\n2. prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go (in-scope) has three RunOptions\n   composite literals:\n     * line 78  — r.resolvePrompt(RunOptions{PromptFile: fixture})\n                  drives Runner.resolvePrompt in runner.go:976.\n                  runner.go is out of scope.\n     * line 85  — defaultResolvePromptForCompare(RunOptions{...})\n                  drives the in-scope helper. CAN be updated.\n     * line 112 — RunViaServiceWith(ctx, svc, dir, RunOptions{...})\n                  drives service_run.go:104. service_run.go's public\n                  API is explicitly named out-of-scope (\"step h\").\n   Even after migrating line 85 and updating line 98's RunBenchmarkWith\n   closure to the new (rcfg, runtime CompareRuntime) shape, the file\n   still carries lines 78 and 112 as pre-existing RunOptions\n   composite literals. runtime-lint scopes diagnostics by file (not\n   by hunk), so the staged file fails the gate on lines that this\n   bead is not authorized to touch.\n\n3. agent_test.go (out of scope, but transitively required) — once\n   RunFunc's signature changes, agent_test.go:584's\n   `func(opts RunOptions) (*Result, error)` no longer matches\n   RunQuorumWithConfig's RunFunc parameter, so the package fails to\n   build. The minimal fix is one line, but agent_test.go has 18\n   other RunOptions composite literals (BuildArgs call sites at\n   lines 118, 130, 148, 157, 165, 188, 196, 209, 214, 677, 684,\n   718, 727, 856, 864, 971, plus 678, 684) that are pre-existing,\n   unrelated to this bead, and depend on BuildArgs (runner.go) being\n   migrated separately. Staging agent_test.go would surface every\n   one of those as a lint violation.\n\nThe runtime-lint hook is mandatory (per project policy: --no-verify is\nforbidden). Diagnostic filtering is whole-file, not hunk-aware, so\n\"only edit my own lines\" doesn't help when the file already contained\nviolations that this bead's named scope can't reach.\n\nWhat a follow-up attempt would need (any of the following)\n\nA. Re-decompose B22d-g to also include claude_stream.go (so\n   resolveClaudeProgressLogDir / runClaudeStreamingFn migrate off\n   RunOptions in the same commit, draining claude_stream_test.go),\n   AND service_run.go's RunViaServiceWith + runner.go's\n   resolvePrompt (so prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go drains), AND\n   agent_test.go's BuildArgs call sites (so the RunFunc signature\n   change compiles cleanly across the package). This is essentially\n   the previously-attempted atomic B22d scope (worker-3f7a's\n   recommendation, applied 2026-04-26 in commit 97561a6e); the\n   subsequent re-decomposition into steps a-h reintroduced this\n   exact tension.\n\nB. Land step h FIRST (and in fact most of the rest of the\n   per-file decomposition) so that claude_stream.go, runner.go,\n   service_run.go, and agent_test.go no longer contain pre-existing\n   RunOptions usages, then run step g — at that point the in-scope\n   test files have no out-of-scope violations and lint passes on the\n   staged set. The current bead-queue ordering puts step g before\n   step h, so this requires a tracker reordering.\n\nC. Change runtime-lint's filter from whole-file to hunk-scoped (so\n   pre-existing violations on lines this bead doesn't touch don't\n   block the commit). This is a lint-policy change in lefthook.yml;\n   not in this bead's scope and not authorized by the bead\n   description.\n\nD. Authorize the worker (model-side) to extend in-scope to cover\n   the transitively-required files (claude_stream.go,\n   runner.go.resolvePrompt + BuildArgs, service_run.go.RunViaServiceWith,\n   and agent_test.go), the same way B22d itself was authorized in\n   2026-04-26 (commit 97561a6e). The diff for that expansion is\n   already prepared and verified locally; only the bead-scope\n   constraint stands in the way.\n\nThe implementation is mechanical and verified locally; the blocker is\nproject-level lint policy versus per-step bead scope, not\nimplementation difficulty. No partial commit is justified, since\nlanding only the in-scope-clean half (compare_adapter.go +\nmodels.go + models_test.go) breaks the package build by leaving\nagent_test.go with an incompatible RunFunc closure, and updating\nthat closure trips the lint on agent_test.go's pre-existing 18+\nRunOptions literals.\nresult_rev=41d464b03c4e5d12b7a9244c3c59ee312e64e255\nbase_rev=41d464b03c4e5d12b7a9244c3c59ee312e64e255\nretry_after=2026-04-27T06:16:44Z","created_at":"2026-04-27T00:16:44.862270268Z","kind":"execute-bead","source":"ddx agent work","summary":"no_changes"}

That commit landed all the\nresolver, schema, and frontend changes B1 specified. The previous attempt at\nthis bead (REVIEW:BLOCK in the bead notes) failed only because the BLOCK was\ndiff-shaped: the reviewer noted that this bead's own diff carried no source\nchanges. The functional contract, however, is met in the working tree, and\nall AC tests pass against HEAD.\n\nPer-AC verification\n-------------------\n1. mediaType=\"image/*\" returns image/png and image/svg+xml artifacts.\n   - Resolver: cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_artifacts.go:51-69\n     translates \"*/*\" suffix to a HasPrefix match.\n   - Test: TestArtifacts_MediaTypeWildcard in\n     cli/internal/server/graphql/artifacts_test.go:161 — PASS.\n\n2. ArtifactSort enum present in schema; each value sorts deterministically\n   with id tie-breaker.\n   - Schema: cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:2097-2109 declares\n     enum ArtifactSort { ID, PATH, MODIFIED, TITLE, DEPS_COUNT }.\n   - Resolver: resolver_artifacts.go:164-197 implements (sortKey, id) tie\n     break in sortArtifacts.\n   - Test: TestArtifacts_SortAllValuesTieBreak (artifacts_test.go:277)\n     exercises every enum value and asserts ascending-id tie-breaker — PASS\n     for all 5 subtests.\n   Note: the bead description proposed enum names PATH_ASC / PATH_DESC /\n   UPDATED_ASC / UPDATED_DESC / STALENESS, but the AC only requires \"enum\n   present with deterministic tie-breaker\" — both shapes satisfy that AC.\n   The shipped enum is the one consumed by the existing frontend\n   (+page.ts:59-65 SORT_OPTIONS) and frontend tests, so changing it now\n   would break downstream B2 work that already depends on it.\n\n3. staleness filter narrows result set.\n   - Resolver: resolver_artifacts.go:72-82 filters by exact staleness\n     equality.\n   - Test: TestArtifacts_StalenessFilter (artifacts_test.go:198) — PASS.\n\n4. Frontend artifacts page calls GraphQL with `search` arg from URL `q`\n   (no client-side filter remaining).\n   - +page.ts:8 declares `$search: String` and passes it to the query.\n   - +page.ts:97 wires `search: q ? q : undefined` into the request.\n   - +page.svelte:55 binds `filtered = $derived(allEdges)` with the comment\n     \"Server-side search: q is sent to the backend so results are correct\n     across all pages, not just the loaded edges.\" There is no client-side\n     substring filter remaining.\n\n5. Changing q/sort/mediaType/staleness clears `after` cursor.\n   - +page.svelte:67-78 navigateWith() rewrites the URL via goto(); the\n     load() function in +page.ts:84 always issues the query with no `after`,\n     so each param change starts a fresh page from the top of the result\n     set. After this change, RunOptions no longer appears in\n    session_index.go (AC #1).\n  - The single existing caller in cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:257\n    (the bead's \"four service-run callers\" overcounts; only one call site exists)\n    was updated to:\n      SessionIndexEntryFromResult(workDir, opts.Harness, opts.Model,\n                                  opts.Provider, opts.Effort, opts.Correlation,\n                                  result, start, finishedAt)\n  - cli/internal/agent/session_index_test.go (TestSessionIndexPreservesWorker-\n    IDCorrelation, line ~195) was updated to call the new signature with\n    discrete args. The test passes.\n  - `cd cli && go build ./...` passes (AC #2).\n  - `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/...` runs clean for everything that\n    isn't TestIntegration_CodexEcho. That test fails for an unrelated reason\n    (real codex CLI returns \"thread not found\") on this machine — it is not\n    a regression introduced by this change, and -short does not skip it.\n\nWhat is blocking\n- The lefthook pre-commit `runtime-lint` rule (lefthook.yml) runs the\n  SD-024 runtimelint analyzer and filters its diagnostics down to the set\n  of staged files. Because the bead's in-scope files\n  (cli/internal/agent/service_run.go, cli/internal/agent/session_index_test.go)\n  contain many out-of-scope RunOptions usages — RunViaService,\n  runFixtureHarnessViaRunner, RunWithConfigViaService, RunViaServiceWith,\n  the `opts := RunOptions{...}` composite at service_run.go:82, and the\n  TestRunViaServiceWithAppendsOneSessionIndexRow composite at\n  session_index_test.go:274 — the lint blocks the commit even though none\n  of those usages are touched by this bead.\n- The bead's stated out-of-scope explicitly excludes \"service_run.go public\n  API deletions (step h)\" (per the parent step-d bead description). Those\n  call sites and signatures are what the lint is flagging.\n- Running the analyzer over ./internal/agent/... reports many other\n  RunOptions references across the package (compare_adapter.go,\n  execute_bead.go, execute_bead_review.go, grade.go, runner.go,\n  agent_runner.go, agent_runner_service.go, claude_stream.go, models.go,\n  etc.) that depend on later B22d steps and the broader Stage-4 retirement.\n- Per project policy, --no-verify / hook-skipping is forbidden; per the\n  bead instructions, modifying out-of-scope files is forbidden. There is no\n  in-scope way to make the lint pass on the staged-file set.\n\nWhat a follow-up attempt would need\n- Land step h (or the broader RunOptions retirement) FIRST so that\n  service_run.go and session_index_test.go no longer contain pre-existing\n  RunOptions usages, then this bead can run and the lint will pass on the\n  staged-file set; OR\n- Re-decompose the lint scope: change runtime-lint in lefthook.yml to\n  filter diagnostics to only the staged hunk lines (e.g. via a diff-aware\n  pass) rather than the whole staged file, so a partial migration step\n  isn't blocked by file-level peers; OR\n- Expand this bead's in-scope to include the full RunOptions removal from\n  service_run.go and session_index_test.go (effectively folding step h into\n  step f), and re-queue with a model that can carry the broader change.\n\nThe diff that was prepared and then reverted is reproducible from the bead\ndescription in 5 minutes; the blocker is project-level lint policy versus\nbead scope, not implementation difficulty.\nresult_rev=4267be6ca4757afe774b14f548a08556dbfbcf6d\nbase_rev=4267be6ca4757afe774b14f548a08556dbfbcf6d\nretry_after=2026-04-27T06:08:19Z","created_at":"2026-04-27T00:08:19.395075757Z","kind":"execute-bead","source":"ddx agent work","summary":"no_changes"}

.ddx/attachments/ddx-b790449b/events.jsonl:3:{"actor":"ddx","body":"Bead ddx-b790449b — no changes this attempt.\n\n## Why this bead was not advanced in one execute-bead pass\n\nThe bead asks for `ddx work` to auto-route execution without any project\nconfig. Read literally, the eight acceptance criteria require:\n\n  AC1  Zero-config drain succeeds (project-side change + upstream relax)\n  AC2  Reword the under-specified error to point at the global config\n  AC3  Enable the triage complexity gate by default + opt-out flag\n  AC4  Auto-tier selection from bead metadata (labels/kind/scope)\n  AC5  Coarse-bead handling: decompose, OR emit a NEW `needs_triage`\n       execution status (distinct from `execution_failed`)\n  AC6  Failure recovery: retry-in-tier vs escalate-to-next-tier\n  AC7  New integration test with a temp project + minimal global config\n  AC8  Doc update presenting zero-config flow as the default\n\nEach of AC3, AC4, and AC5 is a feature-sized piece of work on its own:\n\n  - AC4 (auto-tier selection) implements a tier-inference engine over\n    bead metadata. There is no such engine today; routing currently\n    accepts operator intent or rejects with `routing under-specified`.\n    This overlaps directly with the open endpoint-first routing redesign\n    (memory: project_endpoint_routing_design — 2026-04-21: kill\n    named-provider profiles, move to endpoints + live discovery + fuzzy\n    model match). Implementing tier inference now without aligning with\n    that redesign would either be redone or block it.\n\n  - AC5 (`needs_triage` status) introduces a new ExecuteBeadStatus value\n    that cuts across:\n      internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go     (status enum + classify)\n      internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go       (loop handling + retry)\n      internal/bead/store.go                    (close vs unclaim policy)\n      cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop.go             (operator output)\n      docs/                                     (status table)\n    plus the matching test surface. This cannot share a commit with the\n    routing fix without conflating two reviewable changes.\n\n  - AC3 (triage gate default-on) is a small toggle, but the gate\n    function `w.ComplexityGate` is currently injected from outside and\n    is `nil` in the CLI path (internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:506).\n    Enabling it by default requires either a default ComplexityGate\n    implementation (which is itself a dispatcher into AC4 inference) or\n    a stub that always returns `shouldClaim=true` — the latter is\n    indistinguishable from disabled and would not satisfy the AC.\n\nThe `routing under-specified` error itself is enforced in the upstream\nfizeau library at:\n\n  $GOMODCACHE/.../fizeau@v0.9.28/service_execute.go:192\n\nDDx is currently on github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau v0.9.28. The\nshort-circuit fires when *all* of Harness, Profile, ModelRef, Model,\nProvider are empty. AC1 cannot be met purely on the DDx side without\neither (a) populating one of those fields from a sensible default\n(which is AC4) or (b) a fizeau release that lets the engine's\nResolveRoute see a \"global default_provider\" hint. Per memory\n(feedback_no_local_replace), DDx must always import a properly tagged\nagent release, so a `replace` directive is not available even\ntemporarily.\n\n## What was verified before stopping\n\n  - The under-specified error string is upstream-only — it does not\n    occur in any DDx Go file (Grep across cli/, internal/, cmd/).\n  - The flag set on `ddx agent work` already exposes --profile,\n    --provider, --harness, --model, --model-ref, --effort. The user's\n    report shows `--profile cheap` still failing, which is a separate\n    bug from the bead's main thrust: profile is set as a non-empty\n    flag default (agent.DefaultRoutingProfile, agent_cmd.go:1563) but\n    is being passed through `singleTierAttempt` which uses\n    CLIOverrides without setting Profile (agent_cmd.go:1716–1725).\n    That single-line gap is plausibly the proximate cause of the\n    `--profile cheap` regression but solving it does not satisfy the\n    broader bead.\n  - Triage gate disabled-by-default is at execute_bead_loop.go:506;\n    the gate is a function field on ExecuteBeadWorker. There is no\n    in-tree default implementation.\n\n## Suggested decomposition\n\nRecommend splitting this bead into the following children before any\nimplementation pass attempts AC1:\n\n  ddx-XXXX  Profile flag passthrough fix: ensure --profile/--provider/\n            --model-ref reach singleTierAttempt's CLIOverrides\n            (single small commit + regression test). Likely closes the\n            `--profile cheap` workaround failure.\n\n  ddx-XXXX  Improve under-specified error: when global agent config has\n            providers but the request has no routing inputs, point the\n            operator at the global default_provider rather than at the\n            per-project flag list. Coordinate with fizeau release.\n\n  ddx-XXXX  Tier inference engine over bead metadata\n            (kind/labels/file-touch estimate). Aligns with the\n            endpoint-first routing redesign.\n\n  ddx-XXXX  ExecuteBeadStatusNeedsTriage status + classify rules +\n            close/unclaim policy + tests.\n\n  ddx-XXXX  Default-on ComplexityGate implementation (depends on tier\n            inference) + opt-out flag --no-triage-gate.\n\n  ddx-XXXX  Zero-config integration test (depends on the above).\n\n  ddx-XXXX  Doc update: getting-started zero-config flow.\n\n## What a follow-up attempt needs\n\nIf a single follow-up attempt is desired, it should narrow the bead to\njust AC1 + AC2 with an explicit hand-off: the agent picks a default\nharness/provider from the global agent config when nothing else is set,\ntreating the absence of project config as \"use global default_provider\"\nrather than as a hard error. AC3–AC8 should be filed as sibling beads.\n\nPer the bead's own AC #5, a coarse bead that no tier can confidently\nexecute is supposed to surface for operator triage rather than burn a\ndispatch slot. This rationale is exactly that signal.\nrationale: Bead ddx-b790449b — no changes this attempt.\n\n## Why this bead was not advanced in one execute-bead pass\n\nThe bead asks for `ddx work` to auto-route execution without any project\nconfig. .ddx/attachments/ddx-88e91fdb/events.jsonl:3:{"actor":"ddx","body":"Bead: ddx-88e91fdb — Add chat interface to project home page\n\nDisposition: no commit this pass; recommend decomposing into 4–5 smaller beads.\n\n(a) What is done in this pass\n- Read the bead description and 12 acceptance criteria.\n- Mapped the existing surface area:\n  - GraphQL schema at cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql with gqlgen-generated resolvers; subscription pattern established in resolver_sub_worker.go / resolver_sub_bead.go / resolver_sub_exec.go (buffered channel + ctx-cancel goroutine).\n  - Frontend GraphQL via graphql-request (queries) + graphql-ws (subscriptions) — see cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/gql/{client.ts,subscriptions.ts}. No Houdini.\n  - Project home page at cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/+page.svelte (queue summary + 3 action cards). This is the host surface for the chat pane.\n  - Sessions index at cli/internal/agent/session_index.go, sharded JSONL under .ddx/sessions/ (monthly). agentSessions GraphQL query already exists. This is the linkage target for AC5.\n  - Agent execution entry at cli/internal/agent/service_run.go (RunWithConfigViaService → executeOnService → records a session-index row). Streaming today is per-harness; the work streams events via worker progress, not per-prompt chunks.\n  - Playwright e2e pattern in cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/**/*.e2e.ts uses page.route('/graphql') interception with fixture data; no live backend.\n\n(b) What is blocking a one-pass implementation\nThe bead bundles a feature that fans out across at least 5 independently-reviewable surfaces:\n\n  1. Storage layer (.ddx/chat/<thread-id>/{manifest.json,messages.jsonl}) — new Go package with tests, append-only invariants, project/operator scoping.\n  2. GraphQL schema + resolvers — chatThreads/chatThread/chatMessages queries, chatThreadCreate/chatSend mutations, chatStream subscription. Requires schema.graphql edits, gqlgen regeneration of generated.go, models.go updates, four new resolver files, integration tests.\n  3. service.Execute integration — chatSend must dispatch a session and stream chunks + tool calls back over the chatStream subscription. Today service.Execute is request/response with a non-streaming Result; per-chunk streaming over the subscription bus is new infrastructure. Either: (i) add a streaming variant of executeOnService, or (ii) tail the session's evidence stream and re-emit. Both have non-trivial design choices that belong in the design note before code lands.\n  4. Frontend — chat pane component, thread list, harness/profile picker, streaming render, tool-call inline rendering, Stop button (AC7), cumulative cost header (AC5/AC8). Real-time updates via subscribeChatMessages helper. Roughly parallel in size to the workers detail page.\n  5. Auth posture middleware (AC9) — even as a \"single-operator localhost / tsnet for v1\" stance, the design note must commit to a posture and a route-gate stub must exist so future auth is schema-local. The bead description flags this as load-bearing for the \"remote ops\" framing.\n  6. Design note in docs/helix/02-design/ answering the six subsidiary questions (persistence scope, harness selection, cost accountability, tool-execution scope, concurrency, auth). The bead's AC1 makes this a hard prerequisite — the implementation PR is supposed to *link* to a landed design note. The note must precede or accompany the implementation; it cannot be a postscript.\n  7. Playwright e2e (AC10) — five-step scenario including two browser contexts on the same thread, cancel mid-flight, streamed chunk ordering. Mocking the GraphQL subscription side of this is non-trivial; the existing e2e suite mocks queries via page.route but does not exercise graphql-ws subscriptions.\n\nA single execute-bead commit covering all of the above would either (i) be partial across most of those surfaces and red, or (ii) take many hours of focused work and produce a sprawling diff that the review gate cannot meaningfully verify in one pass. Both outcomes are worse than a clean decomposition.\n\nAdditional concrete unknowns the design note must resolve before code can land cleanly:\n- Is the chatStream subscription chunk-level (assistant text deltas) or event-level (one event per tool call + one final assistant message)? This determines whether service.Execute needs a new streaming surface or can be wrapped post-hoc.\n- For multi-operator on the same thread (AC6), is the second operator a read-only observer or an equal participant who can also send? The bead defaults to \"two browsers on the same thread see each other within 2s\" which implies multi-participant — but the cost-accountability and session-attribution model needs a per-message author identity that today's auth posture (single-operator) does not surface.\n- Does chatSend run in the project worktree or a per-thread worktree? Bead suggests \"same as ddx agent run\" as the default, but if two threads chatSend concurrently they will race the worktree. This argues for per-thread worktree, which is a real cost (disk, fs setup) and belongs in the design note.\n\n(c) What a follow-up attempt would need\n\nRecommend splitting this bead into the following sub-beads, executed in order:\n\n  Sub-bead 1: Design note (docs/helix/02-design/chat-interface.md)\n    - Picks Option A vs B vs C vs D and locks it.\n    - Answers the six subsidiary questions.\n    - Specifies the message/thread schema (manifest.json shape, messages.jsonl entry shape).\n    - Specifies whether chatStream is chunk-level or event-level.\n    - Specifies worktree scoping per thread.\n    - Specifies auth posture for v1 and the gap to broader deployment.\n    Size: 1 pass, design only. No code.\n\n  Sub-bead 2: Chat thread storage package (cli/internal/chat/)\n    - New Go package: ChatThread, ChatMessage, AppendMessage, LoadThread, ListThreads.\n    - Append-only messages.jsonl invariants, atomic manifest writes.\n    - Unit tests including concurrent append safety.\n    Size: 1 pass.\n\n  Sub-bead 3: GraphQL surface (schema + resolvers + integration tests)\n    - schema.graphql edits, gqlgen regen, four resolver files, integration tests.\n    - chatSend dispatches via service.Execute and writes the assistant turn as a single message once the session completes (event-level streaming, simplest first cut). Chunk-level streaming is a follow-up.\n    - chatStream subscription emits message-created events.\n    - Session linkage via SessionIndexEntry.id reference on each assistant message.\n    Size: 1 pass.\n\n  Sub-bead 4: Frontend chat pane\n    - New ChatPane.svelte component, thread list, message list, input box, harness/profile picker.\n    - Mounted on project home page below the actions section.\n    - subscribeChatMessages helper in src/lib/gql/subscriptions.ts.\n    - Cost rollup in thread header. Stop button calls workerStop on the in-flight session's worker.\n    Size: 1 pass.\n\n  Sub-bead 5: Playwright e2e + auth posture middleware\n    - Two-context test, cancel test, streaming chunk test (with mocked graphql-ws server).\n    - Auth middleware gate stub on chat routes.\n    Size: 1 pass.\n\nThe bead's AC11 (depends-on ddx-2513f08c, ddx-2ceb02fa) was not verified in this pass. Sub-bead 1 should re-verify those deps before locking the design.\n\nRecommendation: re-queue this bead as a tracking parent and create the five sub-beads above. The current bead description is unusually thorough and most of its content can be lifted verbatim into the design-note sub-bead's body.\nrationale: Bead: ddx-88e91fdb — Add chat interface to project home page\n\nDisposition: no commit this pass; recommend decomposing into 4–5 smaller beads.\n\n(a) What is done in this pass\n- Read the bead description and 12 acceptance criteria.\n- Mapped the existing surface area:\n  - GraphQL schema at cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql with gqlgen-generated resolvers; subscription pattern established in resolver_sub_worker.go / resolver_sub_bead.go / resolver_sub_exec.go (buffered channel + ctx-cancel goroutine).\n  - Frontend GraphQL via graphql-request (queries) + graphql-ws (subscriptions) — see cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/gql/{client.ts,subscriptions.ts}. No Houdini.\n  - Project home page at cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/+page.svelte (queue summary + 3 action cards). This is the host surface for the chat pane.\n  - Sessions index at cli/internal/agent/session_index.go, sharded JSONL under .ddx/sessions/ (monthly). agentSessions GraphQL query already exists. This is the linkage target for AC5.\n  - Agent execution entry at cli/internal/agent/service_run.go (RunWithConfigViaService → executeOnService → records a session-index row). Streaming today is per-harness; the work streams events via worker progress, not per-prompt chunks.\n  - Playwright e2e pattern in cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/**/*.e2e.ts uses page.route('/graphql') interception with fixture data; no live backend.\n\n(b) What is blocking a one-pass implementation\nThe bead bundles a feature that fans out across at least 5 independently-reviewable surfaces:\n\n  1. Storage layer (.ddx/chat/<thread-id>/{manifest.json,messages.jsonl}) — new Go package with tests, append-only invariants, project/operator scoping.\n  2. Requires schema.graphql edits, gqlgen regeneration of generated.go, models.go updates, four new resolver files, integration tests.\n  3. service.Execute integration — chatSend must dispatch a session and stream chunks + tool calls back over the chatStream subscription. Today service.Execute is request/response with a non-streaming Result; per-chunk streaming over the subscription bus is new infrastructure. Either: (i) add a streaming variant of executeOnService, or (ii) tail the session's evidence stream and re-emit. Both have non-trivial design choices that belong in the design note before code lands.\n  4. Frontend — chat pane component, thread list, harness/profile picker, streaming render, tool-call inline rendering, Stop button (AC7), cumulative cost header (AC5/AC8). Real-time updates via subscribeChatMessages helper. Roughly parallel in size to the workers detail page.\n  5. Auth posture middleware (AC9) — even as a \"single-operator localhost / tsnet for v1\" stance, the design note must commit to a posture and a route-gate stub must exist so future auth is schema-local. The bead description flags this as load-bearing for the \"remote ops\" framing.\n  6. Design note in docs/helix/02-design/ answering the six subsidiary questions (persistence scope, harness selection, cost accountability, tool-execution scope, concurrency, auth). The bead's AC1 makes this a hard prerequisite — the implementation PR is supposed to *link* to a landed design note. The note must precede or accompany the implementation; it cannot be a postscript.\n  7. Playwright e2e (AC10) — five-step scenario including two browser contexts on the same thread, cancel mid-flight, streamed chunk ordering. Mocking the GraphQL subscription side of this is non-trivial; the existing e2e suite mocks queries via page.route but does not exercise graphql-ws subscriptions.\n\nA single execute-bead commit covering all of the above would either (i) be partial across most of those surfaces and red, or (ii) take many hours of focused work and produce a sprawling diff that the review gate cannot meaningfully verify in one pass.
